data.title: Implement scan-config-keys.sh (4-location config key gap scan, makes RED test GREEN)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## What
Implement `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh` so the RED test from Task e4cf-92a3 passes. The script scans 4 locations for config key reads and emits a gap list relative to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md.

## Behavior (Given / When / Then)
- Given a target repo root passed as arg \$1 (defaults to \$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel))
- When invoked, the script must scan ALL of the following sources within that root:
  1. All files under `plugins/dso/` for `read-config.sh <key>` invocations
  2. All files under `plugins/dso/` AND `.claude/` for inline `_read_config_key \"<key>\"` calls
  3. All files under `plugins/dso/` AND `.claude/` for raw grep/cut patterns against `dso-config.conf` (e.g., `grep '^namespace.key=' .claude/dso-config.conf`)
  4. All `^KEY=VALUE` lines in `.claude/dso-config.conf` and any example config file (e.g., `plugins/dso/templates/dso-config.conf.example` if present)
- Then the script must (1) exit 0; (2) print to stdout the sorted unique list of config keys found in scan locations that are NOT documented in `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md`

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh` (create) — implementation
- `.test-index` (modify) — remove RED marker `[test_scan_config_keys_gap_list]`

## Implementation notes
- Use `grep -rE` with appropriate patterns; union results; sort -u
- A key is 'documented' if its literal text appears in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (exact substring match is sufficient)
- Script must be executable (`chmod +x`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh
- [ ] RED test now passes (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-scan-config-keys.sh
- [ ] .test-index RED marker removed
  Verify: ! grep -q '\[test_scan_config_keys_gap_list\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] Invoking the script on the actual repo produces deterministic output and exits 0
  Verify: bash plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh >/dev/null
